ace: noun. A playing card showing a single pip, typically the highest or lowest ranking card in a game.
acre: noun. An English unit of land area (symbol: a. or ac.) originally denoting a day 's ploughing for a yoke of oxen, now standardized as 4,840
air: noun. The substance constituting Earth 's atmosphere: a gaseous mixture of nitrogen, oxygen, and various trace gases.
ape: noun. An anthropoid of the superfamily Hominoidea, generally larger than monkeys and distinguished from them by having no tail.
arc: noun. That part of a circle which a heavenly body appears to pass through as it moves above and below the horizon.
are: verb. second-person singular simple present of be
areic: adjective. Of or pertaining to area; especially used to describe a measurement per unit area.
arf: interjection. Synonym of woof (“sound of a dog's bark”).
cap: noun. A close-fitting hat, either brimless or peaked.
cape: noun. A piece or point of land, extending beyond the adjacent coast into a sea or lake; a promontory; a headland.
caper: noun. A playful leap or jump.
car: noun. A wheeled vehicle that moves independently, with at least three wheels, powered mechanically, steered by a driver and mostly for
carp: noun. Any of various freshwater fish of the family Cyprinidae.
cire: noun. A fabric with a glazed finish.
crap: noun. The husk of grain; chaff.
crape: noun. Alternative form of crepe (“a thin fabric, paper, or pancake”).
ear: noun. The organ of hearing, consisting of the pinna or auricle, auditory canal, eardrum, malleus, incus, stapes and cochlea.
epic: noun. An extended narrative poem in elevated or dignified language, celebrating the feats of a deity, demigod (heroic epic), other legend
era: noun. A time period of indeterminate length, generally more than one year.
erica: noun. Any of many heathers, of the genus Erica, used as garden plants
face: noun. The front part of the head of a human or other animal, featuring the eyes, nose, and mouth, and the surrounding area.
facer: noun. A blow in the face, as in boxing.
fair: adjective. Beautiful, of a pleasing appearance, with a pure and fresh quality.
farce: noun. A style of humor marked by broad improbabilities with little regard to regularity or method.
fare: noun. A going; journey; travel; voyage; course; passage.
fear: noun. A strong, unpleasant emotion or feeling caused by actual or perceived danger or threat.
feria: noun. A weekday on a Church calendar on which no feast is observed.
fiacre: noun. A small horse-drawn carriage for hire; a hackney carriage.
fiar: noun. One in whom the property of an estate is vested, subject to the estate of a liferenter.
fice: noun. Alternative form of feist (“a feist dog”).
fie: interjection. Sometimes followed by on or upon: used to express distaste, disgust, or outrage.
fir: noun. A conifer of the genus Abies.
fire: noun. A (usually self-sustaining) chemical reaction involving the bonding of oxygen with carbon or other fuel, with the production of heat
frap: verb. To draw together tightly; to secure by many turns of a lashing.
icier: adjective. comparative form of icy: more icy
pacifier: noun. Someone or something that pacifies.
pair: noun. Two alike or identical things taken together; often followed by of.
pare: verb. To remove the outer covering or skin of something with a cutting device, typically a knife.
pea: noun. Any of certain plants of the family Fabaceae: Pisum sativum and others.
pear: noun. An edible fruit produced by the pear tree, similar to an apple but typically elongated towards the stem.
peri: noun. A sprite or supernatural being.
pia: noun. The pia mater, the innermost of the meninges that protect the brain and spinal cord.
pic: noun. A picture, especially a photographic image.
pica: noun. A disorder characterized by appetite and craving for non-edible substances, such as chalk, clay, dirt, ice, or sand.
pice: noun. Alternative form of paisa, especially in the form of coins worth 20 cash.
pie: noun. A type of pastry that consists of an outer crust and a filling. (Savory pies are more popular in the UK and sweet pies are more
pier: noun. A raised platform built from the shore out over water, supported on piles; used to secure, or provide access to shipping; a jetty.
price: noun. The cost required to gain possession of something.
race: noun. A contest between people, animals, vehicles, etc. where the goal is to be the first to reach some objective.
rap: noun. A sharp blow with something hard.
rape: noun. The act of forcing sex upon another person without their consent or against their will; originally coitus forced by a man on a woman,
reap: verb. To cut (for example a grain) with a sickle, scythe, or reaping machine.
recap: verb. To seal (something) again with a cap.
ria: noun. A narrow inlet of the sea formed by a flooded unglaciated river valley.
rice: noun. Cereal plants, Oryza sativa of the grass family whose seeds are used as food.
rif: verb. Alternative letter-case form of RIF.
rife: adjective. Widespread, common, prevalent, current (mainly of unpleasant or harmful things).
rip: verb. To divide or separate the parts of (especially something flimsy, such as paper or fabric), by cutting or tearing; to tear off or out
ripe: adjective. Of a fruit, vegetable, seed, etc., ready for reaping or gathering; having attained perfection; mature.
acer: noun. A plant of the genus Acer; a maple.
afer: adjective. African (Used as a specific epithet).
capri: noun. A deep shade of sky blue between cyan and azure.
cpi: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Chinese Pidgin English.
eir: determiner. Belonging to em, gender-neutral third-person singular possessive adjective, equivalent to the singular their and coordinate
epi: noun. A syringe or autoinjector filled with epinephrine, most frequently used for the treatment of acute allergic reactions to avoid or
eric: noun. A fine paid as compensation for violent crimes.
fra: noun. A title of a friar or monk: brother.
fri: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Western Frisian.
pacier: adjective. comparative form of pacey: more pacey
pcf: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Paliyan.
pci: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Duruwa.
peai: noun. The traditional folk medicine and sorcery of Guyana.
picra: noun. The powder of aloes with canella, formerly officinal, employed as a cathartic.
pir: noun. A Muslim holy man or religious leader, especially Sufi.
prc: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Parachi.
